message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:p>Oxford COVID-19 Database (OxCOVID19 Database) is a comprehensive source of information related to the COVID-19 pandemic. This relational database contains time-series data on epidemiology, government responses, mobility, weather and more across time and space for all countries at the national level, and for more than 50 countries at the regional level. It is curated from a variety of (wherever available) official sources. Its purpose is to facilitate the analysis of the spread of SARS-CoV-2 virus and to assess the effects of non-pharmaceutical interventions to reduce the impact of the pandemic. Our database is a freely available, daily updated tool that provides unified and granular information across geographical regions.</jats:p>
title: OxCOVID19 Database, a multimodal data repository for better understanding the global impact of COVID-19

reference.unstructured: Center for Systems Science and Engineering (CSSE) at Johns Hopkins University. Covid-19 data repository. https://github.com/CSSEGISandData/COVID-19 (2020).

reference.article-title: Epidemiological data from the COVID-19 outbreak, real-time case information

reference.unstructured: The Economist. Tracking COVID-19 excess deaths across countries. https://www.economist.com/graphic-detail/2020/04/16/tracking-covid-19-excess-deaths-across-countries (2020).

Hale, T. , Webster, S. , Petherick, A. , Phillips, T. & Kira, B. Oxford COVID-19 Government Response Tracker. Blavatnik School of Government, https://www.bsg.ox.ac.uk/research/research-projects/coronavirus-government-response-tracker (2020).
Hijmans, R. , Garcia, N. & Wieczorek, J. Global Administrative Areas Database (GADM) version 3.6. http://gadm.org/ (2018).

reference.article-title: Answering the right questions for policymakers on COVID-19
